toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Lock Code Manager

**Lock Code Manager scales down to a simple UI for managing your lock's PIN
codes — and scales up to a programmable, multi-lock, multi-provider PIN
platform with scheduled access, condition-driven activation, and
automation-ready services.** Define your codes once and LCM handles setting,
clearing, and monitoring them on every lock, across providers (Z-Wave, ZHA,
Matter, and more) without you wiring up the differences.

Whether you just want a clean dashboard for your front door or you're rotating
PINs across an Airbnb fleet on a nightly schedule, LCM has you covered.

Features:

- Synchronize PIN codes across multiple locks and providers
- Automatic sync — codes are set and cleared as needed, with retry and
  drift detection
- Condition entities control when a code is active:
  - `calendar` — active during events
  - `binary_sensor` / `switch` / `input_boolean` — active when on
  - `schedule` — active during scheduled times
- [Services and actions](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Services-and-Actions)
  for setting/clearing PINs, attaching condition entities, hard-refreshing
  from the lock, and generating safe random PINs from automations
- [Blueprints](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Blueprints)
  for advanced use cases like usage limiting, calendar-driven PINs, and more
- Dashboard strategies and custom cards for managing codes and viewing lock
  status — from one-line auto-generated dashboards to fully hand-composed
  layouts

Supported lock integrations:

| Integration | Read PINs | Push Updates | Code Events | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Z-Wave][wiki-zwave] | Varies | ✅ | ✅ | Some locks mask PINs |
| [ZHA][wiki-zha] | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | Drift detection fallback if lock lacks programming events |
| [Zigbee2MQTT][wiki-zigbee2mqtt] (MQTT)² | Varies | ✅ | ✅ | Same broker as Z2M; PIN support depends on lock |
| [Matter][wiki-matter] | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | PINs write-only per spec |
| [Schlage WiFi][wiki-schlage] | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | Cloud-based, PINs masked |
| [Akuvox][wiki-akuvox]¹ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Local API, polling-based |
| [Virtual][wiki-virtual]¹ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | For testing only |

¹ Custom integration required ([Local Akuvox][local-akuvox],
[hass-virtual][hass-virtual])

² **Zigbee2MQTT (MQTT)** — Pair the lock in [Zigbee2MQTT][zigbee2mqtt] with PIN/user-code support for your firmware.
The **Code Events** column refers to PIN-used automations from Lock Code Manager’s event entity
(which slots were used to lock/unlock).
Zigbee2MQTT lock/unlock actions with user identification are mapped to code slot events for PIN-used automations.
Configure Home Assistant’s **MQTT** integration on the **same broker** Zigbee2MQTT uses.
The default Zigbee2MQTT base topic `zigbee2mqtt` matches what Lock Code Manager expects unless you customize topics
(`{base_topic}/{friendly_name}/set|get`).
During LCM setup, choose your `lock.*` entity from **MQTT**.
If you rename the device in HA, keep it aligned with the **friendly name** in Zigbee2MQTT.

[zigbee2mqtt]: https://www.zigbee2mqtt.io/
[wiki-akuvox]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Akuvox-integration
[wiki-zigbee2mqtt]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Zigbee2MQTT-integration
[wiki-matter]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Matter-integration
[wiki-schlage]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Schlage-integration
[wiki-virtual]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Virtual-integration
[local-akuvox]: https://github.com/pjaudiomv/hass-local-akuvox
[hass-virtual]: https://github.com/twrecked/hass-virtual
[wiki-zha]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/ZHA-integration
[wiki-zwave]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Z-Wave-integration

Adding support for new lock integrations is straightforward — see the
[Adding a Provider](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Adding-a-Provider)
guide. Contributors welcome!

## Integrations That Cannot Currently Be Supported

Some lock integrations cannot currently be supported due to limitations in their underlying
libraries. See the [wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Integrations)
for details.

## Condition Entity Integrations Not Supported

Some condition entity integrations are not compatible. See the
[wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations)
for details.

## Installation

The best way to install this integration is via HACS.

1. Set up your locks in Home Assistant through a supported integration
   (Z-Wave, ZHA, Matter, Schlage, Zigbee2MQTT/MQTT, etc.)
2. Add this repository as a custom integration repository in HACS
3. Go to Settings > Devices & Services > Add Integration
4. Select Lock Code Manager
5. Follow the prompts - additional information about the configuration options are
   available in the Wiki

## Blueprints

Pre-built automations and templates for common lock management
patterns. See [BLUEPRINTS.md](BLUEPRINTS.md) for full details,
input tables, and import buttons.

### Template blueprints

- **Calendar Condition** — Binary sensor for calendar-based slot access
- **Date Range Condition** — Binary sensor for start/end date access

### Automation blueprints

- **Slot Usage Limiter** — Disable a slot after a set number of uses
- **Calendar PIN Setter** — Extract and set PINs from calendar events
- **Auto Re-lock** — Re-lock after a delay with day/night support
- **Lock on Door Close** — Lock when a door sensor detects closure
- **Slot Usage Notifier** — Notify when a code slot PIN is used
- **Condition Linker** — Assign a condition entity to a slot via UI

## Learn More

Visit the [Wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki) for detailed
documentation including configuration, troubleshooting, dashboard setup, and development guides.

## UI & Dashboards

Lock Code Manager ships custom Lovelace **strategies** (which auto-generate UI
from your config) and custom **cards** (which you compose yourself). Pick the
one that matches how much control you want — from a one-click dashboard that
just works (selectable directly from **Settings → Dashboards → Add Dashboard**
on Home Assistant 2026.5+) to a hand-authored layout that places exactly what
you want where you want it.

- [UI overview & decision guide][wiki-ui-overview] — start here
- [UI Strategies][wiki-ui-strategies] — dashboard, view, and section strategies
- [Custom Cards][wiki-ui-cards] — slot card, lock-codes card, and code-display modes

[wiki-ui-overview]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Add-a-UI-for-lock-code-management
[wiki-ui-strategies]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/UI-Strategies
[wiki-ui-cards]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Custom-Cards

## Inspiration

I am a [keymaster](https://github.com/FutureTense/keymaster) maintainer,
and what I learned working on it, and the regular complaints users had about it generating
too many automations, entities, etc. led me to take a different approach. This isn't a
knock on `keymaster`, unfortunately a lot of what is built in this integration wasn't
possible for most of `keymaster`'s life. I briefly considered implementing this into
`keymaster` but:

1. `keymaster` is still a great solution that works as is.
2. `keymaster` is surprisingly simple under the hood because it makes Home Assistant do a
   lot of the heavy lifting for figuring out when to enable and disable a usercode. This
   integration, on the other hand, attempts to do all of the heavy lifting internally in
   code, which means it will generate less entities and automations but it is likely more
   fragile to changes in HA Core or even changes in the codebase.
3. It would be impossible to seamlessly migrate users from the current implementation of
   `keymaster` to this integration's implementation. Rewriting `keymaster` to do this
   would have been the equivalent of creating a new integration anyway.

## Thanks

A big thank you to the other `keymaster` maintainers:

- @FutureTense
- @firstof9

As well as the person who created the base concept that `keymaster` evolved from:
@ptdalen

content.content:
diff --git a/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md b/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md
index fc0fbc7..ea05459 100644
--- a/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md
+++ b/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md
@@ -9,20 +9,40 @@ For supported entity types and useful integrations, see [[Using Condition Entiti
 
 **Integration:** [nielsfaber/scheduler-component](https://github.com/nielsfaber/scheduler-component)
 
-**Why it's not supported:**
-
-The scheduler-component is designed for **task execution** - triggering actions at specific times.
-Lock Code Manager needs **time windows** - periods when access is allowed.
-
-| Scheduler State | What It Means | Access Control? |
-|-----------------|---------------|-----------------|
-| `on` | Schedule is enabled, waiting for its trigger time | Does not mean "allow access now" |
-| `triggered` | The scheduled action just executed | Does not mean "access window is open" |
-| `off` | Schedule is disabled | Wrong context |
-| `completed` | Schedule finished (single-run or paused) | Wrong context |
-
-**What to use instead:** See [[Using Condition Entities|Using-Condition-Entities]] for supported
-alternatives like the native [Schedule helper](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/schedule/).
+**Why its entities don't work as LCM condition entities:**
+
+scheduler-component creates `switch.schedule_<token>` entities whose state tracks the
+*timer's* runtime, not whether access should be allowed right now. LCM condition entities
+need a state that means "access is allowed" when `on` and "access is denied" when `off`.
+
+| Scheduler State | What It Means | Why It Fails as a Condition |
+|-----------------|---------------|------------------------------|
+| `on` | Timer is armed, waiting for its next trigger time | Means "scheduled", not "active window" |
+| `triggered` | An action just fired (entity resets after ~1 min) | Momentary state, not a window |
+| `off` | Schedule is disabled entirely | Wrong axis — schedule on/off, not window open/closed |
+| `unknown` | Schedule failed to initialize | Error state |
+
+A scheduler entity reporting `on` at 3pm tells you "this schedule is waiting to fire at
+some future time", not "access is currently permitted" — so attaching it as an LCM
+condition entity produces incorrect behavior.
+
+**Using scheduler-component with LCM anyway:**
+
+scheduler-component *can* be used alongside LCM by skipping LCM's condition entity
+system entirely and instead having scheduler-component **drive the slot's Enabled
+switch directly**. Each timeslot in scheduler-component specifies free-form `actions`
+(a list of HA service calls), so you can configure a schedule whose action calls
+`switch.turn_on` on the slot's Enabled switch at the window start and
+`switch.turn_off` at the window stop. Any gating logic — day-of-week, presence,
+device state — lives inside scheduler-component's own `weekdays` / `conditions`
+config. From LCM's perspective the slot simply gets enabled and disabled like any
+other switch toggle; LCM never sees the scheduler entity at all.
+
+**What to use instead (if you want LCM condition entities):**
+
+For native LCM condition entity support, see [[Using Condition Entities|Using-Condition-Entities]].
+The built-in [Schedule helper](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/schedule/) is
+the recommended primitive — its `on` / `off` states correctly model "in window" / "out of window".
 
 ## Adding New Integrations to This List

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs: clarify scheduler-component limitation is condition-entity-only

The previous wording implied scheduler-component is fully incompatible with LCM.
In practice the limitation is narrower: scheduler entities can't be used as LCM
condition entities (their states track timer runtime, not access windows), but
the integration can still drive slot Enabled switches directly from its own
schedules. Point users at the wiki for the workaround details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1 --oneline

content.subject: Research scheduler-component to understand mechanics
content.description: Read nielsfaber/scheduler-component README and docs to understand: entities it creates, how schedules trigger actions, what targets/services it can call, and whether it can directly toggle switches like LCM's slot enabled switch

content.subject: Revise wiki draft for scheduler-component section
content.description: Update Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md scheduler-component section: scope rationale to entity-as-condition-entity use, add workaround section explaining how to drive slot enabled toggle from scheduler-component schedules

content.subject: Revise README draft for condition entity integrations
content.description: Update README.md "Condition Entity Integrations Not Supported" section to clarify the nuance: entity-state semantics are incompatible, but integrations can still drive slot enabled toggles via automation

content.subject: Apply approved changes and open README PR
content.description: Once drafts approved: commit wiki change to wiki repo (master), create branch + commit + push + PR for README change using project PR template

content.text: Creating the PR with the project template.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "docs: clarify scheduler-component limitation is condition-entity-only" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

The README's "Condition Entity Integrations Not Supported" section currently implies scheduler-component (and similar integrations) are fully incompatible with Lock Code Manager. In practice the limitation is narrower: scheduler-component's entities can't be used as LCM **condition entities** (their `on`/`triggered`/`off`/`unknown` states track the timer's runtime, not whether an access window is currently open), but the integration itself can still drive slot Enabled switches directly from its own schedule actions — bypassing LCM's condition entity system entirely.

This PR updates the README blurb to:
- explicitly scope the limitation to condition-entity semantics
- signal that the underlying integration may still be usable via slot Enabled toggling
- point readers at the wiki where the full scheduler-component workaround is now documented

The companion wiki update lives in [`Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md`](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations) and was pushed alongside this PR.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR fixes or closes issue: n/a
- This PR is related to issue: n/a

Docs-only change. No code or behavior impact.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The wiki repo is a separate git repo (lives at `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager.wiki/`, default branch `master`, not `main`) that GitHub auto-publishes on push — no PR workflow needed. Worth knowing for future wiki edits.
- The PR template's "Breaking change" section was correctly omitted because the project's auto-tagger keys on section *presence* (per the breaking-change feedback memory) — adding an empty/N-A heading would have been miscategorized as breaking.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**Shipped:**
- **Wiki**: `Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations.md` — committed `3ec3ee2` and pushed to `master`. Already live at https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations
- **README PR**: [#1131 — docs: clarify scheduler-component limitation is condition-entity-only](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/pull/1131) on branch `docs/clarify-scheduler-condition-entity`, using the project template, classified as "Code quality improvements", no Breaking Change section.

The "1 uncommitted change" warning from `gh pr create` is the pre-existing untracked `.devcontainer/devcontainer-lock.json` — unrelated to this PR.

lastPrompt: check the open PRs and see why CI failed. If we can resolve them for the dependency bump, let's do it and fix the PR in that branch. Otherwise lets close the PR

aiTitle: Clarify scheduler component entity limitation in docs

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **#1127** (HA group): The failure is `AttributeError: type object 'HeimanCluster' has no attribute 'Commands'` inside the bundled `homeassistant.components.matter.button` — i.e., the new HA version is incompatible with the chip-clusters / matter-server version that resolves alongside it. This is a dependency-internal mismatch, not our code, so the fix (if any) is to also bump matter-related pins in our requirements.
- **#1126** (mypy 2.0): One real type error in our code, `zigbee2mqtt.py:281` — `Any | None` assigned to `int`. Mypy 2.0 just tightened — this is a genuine type-narrowing bug to fix.
- **#1125** (babel/preset-env): Looking at the log, the actual blocker is "Repo is dirty after build" — the new babel produces a slightly different bundled `lock-code-manager.js` than what's checked in. Just need to run `yarn build` locally and commit. (The TS warnings shown are pre-existing in main, not caused by this PR.)
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me get more detail on each before deciding what's fixable in-branch.

content.content:
{"body":"Updates the requirements on [pytest](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest), [pytest-homeassistant-custom-component](https://github.com/MatthewFlamm/pytest-homeassistant-custom-component), [homeassistant](https://github.com/home-assistant/core) and [zwave-js-server-python](https://github.com/home-assistant-libs/zwave-js-server-python) to permit the latest version. Updates `pytest` to 9.0.3
Release notes

Sourced from pytest's releases.

9.0.3

pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07)

Bug fixes

  • #12444: Fixed pytest.approx which now correctly takes into account ~collections.abc.Mapping keys order to compare them.

  • #13634: Blocking a conftest.py file using the -p no: option is now explicitly disallowed.

    Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading.

    Pytest now raises a clear UsageError explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via -p.

  • #13734: Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with __tracebackhide__ = True.

  • #14195: Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed.

  • #14343: Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176).

Improved documentation

  • #13388: Clarified documentation for -p vs PYTEST_PLUGINS plugin loading and fixed an incorrect -p example.
  • #13731: Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. capsys and capfd) take precedence over the -s / --capture=no command-line options in Accessing captured output from a test function <accessing-captured-output>.
  • #14088: Clarified that the default pytest_collection hook sets session.items before it calls pytest_collection_finish, not after.
  • #14255: TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation.

Contributor-facing changes

  • #12689: The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible on the web interface.

    -- by aleguy02

Updates `pytest` to 9.0.3
Release notes

Sourced from pytest's releases.

9.0.3

pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07)

Bug fixes

  • #12444: Fixed pytest.approx which now correctly takes into account ~collections.abc.Mapping keys order to compare them.

  • #13634: Blocking a conftest.py file using the -p no: option is now explicitly disallowed.

    Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading.

    Pytest now raises a clear UsageError explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via -p.

  • #13734: Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with __tracebackhide__ = True.

  • #14195: Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed.

  • #14343: Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176).

Improved documentation

  • #13388: Clarified documentation for -p vs PYTEST_PLUGINS plugin loading and fixed an incorrect -p example.
  • #13731: Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. capsys and capfd) take precedence over the -s / --capture=no command-line options in Accessing captured output from a test function <accessing-captured-output>.
  • #14088: Clarified that the default pytest_collection hook sets session.items before it calls pytest_collection_finish, not after.
  • #14255: TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation.

Contributor-facing changes

  • #12689: The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible on the web interface.

    -- by aleguy02

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- PEP 758 (Python 3.14) made `except X, Y:` parse as `except (X, Y):` — parentheses are now optional. So that line is fine on 3.14 (which CI uses); we don't need to touch it. The mypy 2.0 complaint is a real but separate type-narrowing issue.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Handle lock/unlock actions with user identification (keypad PIN usage)\\n        if isinstance(action, str) and action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS:\\n            action_user = payload.get(\\\"action_user\\\")\\n            if action_user is not None and not isinstance(action_user, bool):\\n                try:\\n                    code_slot = int(action_user)\\n                except ValueError, TypeError:\\n                    LOGGER.debug(\\n                        \\\"Ignoring %s with non-numeric action_user %r for %s\\\",\\n                        action,\\n                        action_user,\\n                        self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    )\\n                    return\\n                to_locked = action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_LOCKED\\n                self.async_fire_code_slot_event(\\n                    code_slot=code_slot,\\n                    to_locked=to_locked,\\n                    action_text=action,\\n                    source_data=payload,\\n                )\\n            return\\n\\n        # Handle pin_code added / deleted (Z2M action events, not the users object)\\n        if action in (\\\"pin_code_added\\\", \\\"pin_code_deleted\\\"):\\n            action_user = payload.get(\\\"action_user\\\")\\n            if action_user is not None:\\n                LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"Lock %s received %s for user %s\\\",\\n                    self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    action,\\n                    action_user,\\n                )\\n                if self.coordinator:\\n                    self.hass.async_create_task(\\n                        self.coordinator.async_request_refresh()\\n                    )\\n            return\\n\\n        # Handle users data in state update\\n        users_data = payload.get(\\\"users\\\")\\n        if users_data and isinstance(users_data, dict):\\n            updates: dict[int, str | SlotCode] = {}\\n            for user_id_str, user_info in users_data.items():\\n                try:\\n                    user_id = int(user_id_str)\\n                except ValueError, TypeError:\\n                    LOGGER.warning(\\n                        \\\"Skipping non-numeric Zigbee2MQTT user key %r for %s\\\",\\n                        user_id_str,\\n                        self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    )\\n                    continue\\n\\n                if not isinstance(user_info, dict):\\n                    LOGGER.debug(\\n                        \\\"Skipping unexpected user_info type %s for slot %s on %s\\\",\\n                        type(user_info).__name__,\\n                        user_id_str,\\n                        self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    )\\n                    continue\\n\\n                status = user_info.get(\\\"status\\\")\\n                pin_code_present = \\\"pin_code\\\" in user_info\\n                pin_raw = user_info.get(\\\"pin_code\\\")\\n\\n                # Zigbee2MQTT often omits pin_code when expose_pin is false (default on\\n                # several Yale models). Treating that as EMPTY makes the coordinator think\\n                # the slot is cleared, so disabling the slot skips clear_usercode while the\\n                # lock still holds the PIN. Only treat as EMPTY when MQTT exposes the field.\\n                if status == \\\"enabled\\\":\\n                    if _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(pin_raw):\\n                        updates[user_id] = str(pin_raw)\\n                    elif pin_code_present:\\n                        updates[user_id] = SlotCode.EMPTY\\n                    else:\\n                        continue\\n                else:\\n                    updates[user_id] = SlotCode.EMPTY\\n\\n            if updates and self.coordinator:\\n                LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"Lock %s received push update for slots: %s\\\",\\n                    self.lock.entity_id,\\n                    list(updates.keys()),\\n                )\\n                self.coordinator.push_update(updates)\\n\\n        # Handle response to get request with pin_code data\\n        pin_code_data = payload.get(\\\"pin_code\\\")\\n        if pin_code_data and isinstance(pin_code_data, dict):\\n            user_id = pin_code_data.get(\\\"user\\\")\\n            if user_id is None:\\n                LOGGER.debug(\\n                    \\\"Ignoring pin_code payload without user field for %s\\\",\\n                    self.lock.entity_id,\\n                )\\n                return\\n\\n            try:\\n                user_id = int(user_id)\\n            except ValueError, TypeError:\\n                LOGGER.warning(\\n                    \\\"Ignoring pin_code payload with non-numeric user for %s\\\",\\n                    self.lock.entity_id,\\n                )\\n                return\\n\\n            if user_id in self._pending_codes:\\n                future = self._pending_codes.pop(user_id)\\n                if not future.done():\\n                    user_enabled = pin_code_data.get(\\\"user_enabled\\\", False)\\n                    pin_code = pin_code_data.get(\\\"pin_code\\\")\\n                    if user_enabled and _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(pin_code):\\n                        future.set_result(str(pin_code))\\n                    else:\\n                        future.set_result(None)\"}]"}

content.subject: Investigate and fix dependency bump PR CI failures
content.description: For each open dependabot PR: determine whether failure is fixable in-branch (#1126 mypy type narrowing, #1125 rebuild yarn bundle, #1127 HA group dependency mismatch). Fix or close per user direction.

lastPrompt: you can also use serena to explore

aiTitle: Clarify scheduler component entity limitation in docs

content.text:
Now I understand the mypy issue. Let me check `_process_z2m_device_payload` carefully to see the variable shadowing:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The method assigns `user_id = int(user_id_str)` inside the `for user_id_str, user_info in users_data.items()` loop (narrowing to `int`). Later in a separate `if pin_code_data` block, it does `user_id = pin_code_data.get("user")` — reassigning the same name to `Any | None`. Mypy 2.0 flags this because the variable was already typed as `int` in scope.
- The fix mirrors the loop's own pattern: use a separate name (e.g. `raw_user`) for the untyped `.get()` result, and only assign `user_id` once we've narrowed it through `int()`. This makes the function's type story uniform: `user_id` is always `int`, never reassigned to a wider type.
- For #1127, the failure is HA's bundled `matter.button` reaching for `HeimanCluster.Commands` — an attribute that needs a newer chip-clusters than what's pinned via `python-matter-server==8.1.2`. The diff doesn't bump matter-server, so the import breaks at HA load time. Fixable only if we also bump matter-server here, or close and let dependabot re-roll.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me check whether a matter-server bump is available, then plan the three fixes.

input.command: curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/python-matter-server/json | python3 -c "import sys, json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('latest:', d['info']['version']); print('current pin:', '8.1.2'); print('releases:', sorted([v for v in d['releases'] if v.startswith('8.') or v.startswith('9.') or v.startswith('10.')])[-10:])"

file.content:
---
# To get started with Dependabot version updates, you'll need to specify which
# package ecosystems to update and where the package manifests are located.
# Please see the documentation for all configuration options:
# https://help.github.com/github/administering-a-repository/configuration-options-for-dependency-updates

version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "github-config"
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "python"
    groups:
      homeassistant:
        patterns:
          - "homeassistant"
          - "python-matter-server"
          - "pytest"
          - "pytest-homeassistant-custom-component"
          - "zeroconf"
          - "zwave-js-server-python"
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: "/" # Location of package manifests
    schedule:
      interval: daily
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "javascript"

input.command: curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/homeassistant/json | python3 -c "import sys, json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('latest:', d['info']['version']); releases = sorted([v for v in d['releases'] if v.startswith('2026.5') or v.startswith('2026.6')]); print('2026.5+/6 releases:', releases[-10:])"

- Added wfsens as a occupancy source in wiz ([@th3spis] - [#166799]) ([wiz docs])
- Fix WiZ Light config flow timeout by properly closing UDP connections ([@robwasripped] - [#168456]) ([wiz docs])
- Fix IntelliFire setup recovery ([@jeeftor] - [#169739]) ([intellifire docs])
- Fix hassio auth IndexError on Supervisor Unix socket requests ([@agners] - [#169911]) ([hassio docs])
- Update gardena ble to 2.8.1 ([@elupus] - [#169914]) ([gardena_bluetooth docs]) ([husqvarna_automower_ble docs]) (dependency)
- Bump serialx to 1.7.1 ([@puddly] - [#169928]) ([serial docs]) ([acer_projector docs]) ([usb docs]) (dependency)
- Bump holidays to 0.96 ([@gjohansson-ST] - [#169939]) ([workday docs]) ([holiday docs]) (dependency)
- Add support for options to todo triggers ([@emontnemery] - [#169947]) ([todo docs])
- Bump pyTibber to 0.37.5 ([@Danielhiversen] - [#169981]) ([tibber docs]) (dependency)
- Bump python-duco-client to 0.4.0 ([@ronaldvdmeer] - [#169776]) ([duco docs]) (dependency)
- Bump python-duco-client to 0.4.1 ([@ronaldvdmeer] - [#169991]) ([duco docs]) (dependency)
- Proper handling of malformed data during FRITZ!Box Tools setup ([@mib1185] - [#170030]) ([fritz docs])
- Fix websocket certificate verification Bump axis to v70 ([@Kane610] - [#170038]) ([axis docs]) (dependency)
- Fix `is_closed` state for DynamicGarageDoor in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170052]) ([overkiz docs])
- Fix tilt controls for TiltOnlyVenetianBlind in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170055]) ([overkiz docs])
- Fix cover controls for UpDownBioclimaticPergola in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170058]) ([overkiz docs])
- Bump pyOverkiz to 1.20.3 ([@iMicknl] - [#170060]) ([overkiz docs]) (dependency)
- Bump deebot-client to 18.3.0 ([@edenhaus] - [#170066]) ([ecovacs docs]) (dependency)
- Set `is_closed` state to `None` when a cover state returns "unknown" in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170081]) ([overkiz docs])
- Fix sensors getting wrong unit from MeasuredValueType attribute in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170088]) ([overkiz docs])
- Fix Z-Wave discovery crash with unknown node firmware version ([@TheJulianJES] - [#170090]) ([zwave_js docs])
- Bump ZHA to 1.3.1 ([@TheJulianJES] - [#170095]) ([zha docs]) (dependency)
- Bump python-bsblan to 5.2.1 ([@liudger] - [#170100]) ([bsblan docs]) (dependency)
- Bump blebox_uniapi to v2.5.3 ([@bkobus-bbx] - [#170115]) ([blebox docs]) (dependency)
- Fix is_closed state for DynamicGate covers in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170130]) ([overkiz docs])
- Fix tilt support for UpDownVenetianBlind (rts:VenetianBlindRTSComponent) in Overkiz ([@iMicknl] - [#170047]) ([overkiz docs])

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Summary:

- **#1126** (mypy 2.0): fixable — type narrowing issue in our code
- **#1125** (babel): fixable — just need to regenerate the bundled `.js`
- **#1127** (HA group): **not fixable** — HA 2026.5.0/2026.5.1's `matter/button.py` references `HeimanCluster.Commands.MutingSensor`, but `HeimanCluster` in every released `python-matter-server` only has `Attributes`, no `Commands` subclass. This is an upstream HA bug independent of our pins. Close it.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Lock Code Manager
2	
3	**Lock Code Manager scales down to a simple UI for managing your lock's PIN
4	codes — and scales up to a programmable, multi-lock, multi-provider PIN
5	platform with scheduled access, condition-driven activation, and
6	automation-ready services.** Define your codes once and LCM handles setting,
7	clearing, and monitoring them on every lock, across providers (Z-Wave, ZHA,
8	Matter, and more) without you wiring up the differences.
9	
10	Whether you just want a clean dashboard for your front door or you're rotating
11	PINs across an Airbnb fleet on a nightly schedule, LCM has you covered.
12	
13	Features:
14	
15	- Synchronize PIN codes across multiple locks and providers
16	- Automatic sync — codes are set and cleared as needed, with retry and
17	  drift detection
18	- Condition entities control when a code is active:
19	  - `calendar` — active during events
20	  - `binary_sensor` / `switch` / `input_boolean` — active when on
21	  - `schedule` — active during scheduled times
22	- [Services and actions](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Services-and-Actions)
23	  for setting/clearing PINs, attaching condition entities, hard-refreshing
24	  from the lock, and generating safe random PINs from automations
25	- [Blueprints](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Blueprints)
26	  for advanced use cases like usage limiting, calendar-driven PINs, and more
27	- Dashboard strategies and custom cards for managing codes and viewing lock
28	  status — from one-line auto-generated dashboards to fully hand-composed
29	  layouts
30	
31	Supported lock integrations:
32	
33	| Integration | Read PINs | Push Updates | Code Events | Notes |
34	| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
35	| [Z-Wave][wiki-zwave] | Varies | ✅ | ✅ | Some locks mask PINs |
36	| [ZHA][wiki-zha] | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | Drift detection fallback if lock lacks programming events |
37	| [Zigbee2MQTT][wiki-zigbee2mqtt] (MQTT)² | Varies | ✅ | ✅ | Same broker as Z2M; PIN support depends on lock |
38	| [Matter][wiki-matter] | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | PINs write-only per spec |
39	| [Schlage WiFi][wiki-schlage] | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | Cloud-based, PINs masked |
40	| [Akuvox][wiki-akuvox]¹ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Local API, polling-based |
41	| [Virtual][wiki-virtual]¹ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | For testing only |
42	
43	¹ Custom integration required ([Local Akuvox][local-akuvox],
44	[hass-virtual][hass-virtual])
45	
46	² **Zigbee2MQTT (MQTT)** — Pair the lock in [Zigbee2MQTT][zigbee2mqtt] with PIN/user-code support for your firmware.
47	The **Code Events** column refers to PIN-used automations from Lock Code Manager’s event entity
48	(which slots were used to lock/unlock).
49	Zigbee2MQTT lock/unlock actions with user identification are mapped to code slot events for PIN-used automations.
50	Configure Home Assistant’s **MQTT** integration on the **same broker** Zigbee2MQTT uses.
51	The default Zigbee2MQTT base topic `zigbee2mqtt` matches what Lock Code Manager expects unless you customize topics
52	(`{base_topic}/{friendly_name}/set|get`).
53	During LCM setup, choose your `lock.*` entity from **MQTT**.
54	If you rename the device in HA, keep it aligned with the **friendly name** in Zigbee2MQTT.
55	
56	[zigbee2mqtt]: https://www.zigbee2mqtt.io/
57	[wiki-akuvox]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Akuvox-integration
58	[wiki-zigbee2mqtt]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Zigbee2MQTT-integration
59	[wiki-matter]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Matter-integration
60	[wiki-schlage]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Schlage-integration
61	[wiki-virtual]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Virtual-integration
62	[local-akuvox]: https://github.com/pjaudiomv/hass-local-akuvox
63	[hass-virtual]: https://github.com/twrecked/hass-virtual
64	[wiki-zha]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/ZHA-integration
65	[wiki-zwave]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Z-Wave-integration
66	
67	Adding support for new lock integrations is straightforward — see the
68	[Adding a Provider](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Adding-a-Provider)
69	guide. Contributors welcome!
70	
71	## Integrations That Cannot Currently Be Supported
72	
73	Some lock integrations cannot currently be supported due to limitations in their underlying
74	libraries. See the [wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Integrations)
75	for details.
76	
77	## Condition Entity Integrations Not Supported
78	
79	Some condition entity integrations are not compatible. See the
80	[wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations)
81	for details.
82	
83	## Installation
84	
85	The best way to install this integration is via HACS.
86	
87	1. Set up your locks in Home Assistant through a supported integration
88	   (Z-Wave, ZHA, Matter, Schlage, Zigbee2MQTT/MQTT, etc.)
89	2. Add this repository as a custom integration repository in HACS
90	3. Go to Settings > Devices & Services > Add Integration
91	4. Select Lock Code Manager
92	5. Follow the prompts - additional information about the configuration options are
93	   available in the Wiki
94	
95	## Blueprints
96	
97	Pre-built automations and templates for common lock management
98	patterns. See [BLUEPRINTS.md](BLUEPRINTS.md) for full details,
99	input tables, and import buttons.
100	
101	### Template blueprints
102	
103	- **Calendar Condition** — Binary sensor for calendar-based slot access
104	- **Date Range Condition** — Binary sensor for start/end date access
105	
106	### Automation blueprints
107	
108	- **Slot Usage Limiter** — Disable a slot after a set number of uses
109	- **Calendar PIN Setter** — Extract and set PINs from calendar events
110	- **Auto Re-lock** — Re-lock after a delay with day/night support
111	- **Lock on Door Close** — Lock when a door sensor detects closure
112	- **Slot Usage Notifier** — Notify when a code slot PIN is used
113	- **Condition Linker** — Assign a condition entity to a slot via UI
114	
115	## Learn More
116	
117	Visit the [Wiki](https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki) for detailed
118	documentation including configuration, troubleshooting, dashboard setup, and development guides.
119	
120	## UI & Dashboards
121	
122	Lock Code Manager ships custom Lovelace **strategies** (which auto-generate UI
123	from your config) and custom **cards** (which you compose yourself). Pick the
124	one that matches how much control you want — from a one-click dashboard that
125	just works (selectable directly from **Settings → Dashboards → Add Dashboard**
126	on Home Assistant 2026.5+) to a hand-authored layout that places exactly what
127	you want where you want it.
128	
129	- [UI overview & decision guide][wiki-ui-overview] — start here
130	- [UI Strategies][wiki-ui-strategies] — dashboard, view, and section strategies
131	- [Custom Cards][wiki-ui-cards] — slot card, lock-codes card, and code-display modes
132	
133	[wiki-ui-overview]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Add-a-UI-for-lock-code-management
134	[wiki-ui-strategies]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/UI-Strategies
135	[wiki-ui-cards]: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/Custom-Cards
136	
137	## Inspiration
138	
139	I am a [keymaster](https://github.com/FutureTense/keymaster) maintainer,
140	and what I learned working on it, and the regular complaints users had about it generating
141	too many automations, entities, etc. led me to take a different approach. This isn't a
142	knock on `keymaster`, unfortunately a lot of what is built in this integration wasn't
143	possible for most of `keymaster`'s life. I briefly considered implementing this into
144	`keymaster` but:
145	
146	1. `keymaster` is still a great solution that works as is.
147	2. `keymaster` is surprisingly simple under the hood because it makes Home Assistant do a
148	   lot of the heavy lifting for figuring out when to enable and disable a usercode. This
149	   integration, on the other hand, attempts to do all of the heavy lifting internally in

git add custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zigbee2mqtt.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(types): rename raw .get() result to keep user_id narrowed for mypy 2.0

Mypy 2.0 flags `user_id = pin_code_data.get("user")` because user_id was
already narrowed to int by an earlier loop body in the same function.
Mirror the loop's own pattern: keep the untyped .get() result under
`raw_user`, and only assign `user_id` via `int(raw_user)`. user_id stays
int throughout the function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin dependabot/pip/mypy-2.0.0 2>&1 | tail -10

1	"""Module for Zigbee2MQTT locks."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import asyncio
6	from collections.abc import Callable
7	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
8	from datetime import timedelta
9	import json
10	from typing import Any, Literal
11	
12	from homeassistant.components.mqtt import (
13	    DOMAIN as MQTT_DOMAIN,
14	    async_publish,
15	    async_subscribe,
16	)
17	from homeassistant.components.mqtt.models import ReceiveMessage
18	from homeassistant.components.mqtt.util import mqtt_config_entry_enabled
19	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
20	from homeassistant.core import callback
21	from homeassistant.exceptions import HomeAssistantError
22	
23	from ..exceptions import LockDisconnected
24	from ..models import SlotCode
25	from ._base import BaseLock
26	from .const import LOGGER
27	
28	# Default Zigbee2MQTT base topic
29	DEFAULT_BASE_TOPIC = "zigbee2mqtt"
30	
31	# Zigbee2MQTT action values for lock/unlock events triggered by PIN entry.
32	# These come from the DoorLock cluster's OperatingEventNotification and
33	# ProgrammingEventNotification via zigbee-herdsman-converters.
34	_Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_LOCKED = frozenset(
35	    {
36	        "lock",
37	        "keypad_lock",
38	        "manual_lock",
39	        "rf_lock",
40	    }
41	)
42	_Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_UNLOCKED = frozenset(
43	    {
44	        "unlock",
45	        "keypad_unlock",
46	        "manual_unlock",
47	        "rf_unlock",
48	    }
49	)
50	_Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS = _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_LOCKED | _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_UNLOCKED
51	
52	
53	def _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(pin_raw: Any) -> bool:
54	    """
55	    Return True when MQTT exposes a usable PIN value (including numeric zero).
56	
57	    Plain truthiness is unsafe: ``0`` is a valid digit and must not be treated as
58	    absent. Boolean JSON values are ignored because they are not PIN payloads.
59	    """
60	    if pin_raw is None:
61	        return False
62	    if isinstance(pin_raw, bool):
63	        return False
64	    if isinstance(pin_raw, str):
65	        return pin_raw.strip() != ""
66	    return str(pin_raw) != ""
67	
68	
69	@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
70	class Zigbee2MQTTLock(BaseLock):
71	    """Class to represent Zigbee2MQTT lock."""
72	
73	    _base_topic: str = field(init=False, default=DEFAULT_BASE_TOPIC)
74	    _friendly_name: str | None = field(init=False, default=None)
75	    _unsubscribe: Callable[[], None] | None = field(init=False, default=None)
76	    _pending_codes: dict[int, asyncio.Future[str | None]] = field(
77	        init=False, default_factory=dict
78	    )
79	
80	    @property
81	    def domain(self) -> str:
82	        """Return integration domain."""
83	        return MQTT_DOMAIN
84	
85	    @property
86	    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
87	        """Return whether this lock supports push-based updates."""
88	        return True
89	
90	    @property
91	    def supports_code_slot_events(self) -> bool:
92	        """Return whether this lock supports code slot events."""
93	        return True
94	
95	    @property
96	    def usercode_scan_interval(self) -> timedelta:
97	        """
98	        Return scan interval for usercodes.
99	
100	        With push updates, we only need polling as a fallback.
101	        """
102	        return timedelta(minutes=5)
103	
104	    @property
105	    def hard_refresh_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
106	        """Return interval for hard refresh."""
107	        return timedelta(hours=1)
108	
109	    @property
110	    def connection_check_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
111	        """Return interval for connection checks."""
112	        return timedelta(seconds=30)
113	
114	    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
115	        """Subscribe to the device topic before the coordinator runs its first poll."""
116	        await self._async_ensure_device_subscription()
117	
118	    def _get_friendly_name(self) -> str | None:
119	        """
120	        Get the Zigbee2MQTT friendly name for this device.
121	
122	        Reads ``device_registry`` name on each call so renames stay aligned with the
123	        Zigbee2MQTT friendly name (cached value alone would go stale).
124	        """
125	        if not self.device_entry:
126	            LOGGER.debug("No device entry for %s", self.lock.entity_id)
127	            return None
128	
129	        # Check if this is a Zigbee2MQTT device by identifiers
130	        is_z2m = any(
131	            len(identifier) >= 2 and str(identifier[1]).startswith("zigbee2mqtt_")
132	            for identifier in self.device_entry.identifiers
133	        )
134	
135	        if not is_z2m:
136	            LOGGER.debug("Device %s is not a Zigbee2MQTT device", self.lock.entity_id)
137	            return None
138	
139	        name = self.device_entry.name
140	        if name != self._friendly_name:
141	            self._friendly_name = name
142	            LOGGER.debug(
143	                "Zigbee2MQTT friendly name for %s: %s",
144	                self.lock.entity_id,
145	                name,
146	            )
147	        return name
148	
149	    def _get_topic(self, suffix: str = "") -> str | None:
150	        """Get the MQTT topic for this device."""
151	        friendly_name = self._get_friendly_name()
152	        if not friendly_name:
153	            return None
154	        if suffix:
155	            return f"{self._base_topic}/{friendly_name}/{suffix}"
156	        return f"{self._base_topic}/{friendly_name}"
157	
158	    def _maybe_raise_wrong_bridge_disconnect(self) -> None:
159	        """Raise when MQTT works but this entity cannot map to a Zigbee2MQTT topic."""
160	        if self.device_entry is None:
161	            return
162	        if any(
163	            len(identifier) >= 2 and str(identifier[1]).startswith("zigbee2mqtt_")
164	            for identifier in self.device_entry.identifiers
165	        ):
166	            return
167	        raise LockDisconnected(
168	            "This entity is not a Zigbee2MQTT lock (device registry lacks a "
169	            "zigbee2mqtt_* identifier)."
170	        )
171	
172	    async def async_is_integration_connected(self) -> bool:
173	        """Return whether MQTT is usable and this lock maps to a Z2M device topic."""
174	        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
175	            return False
176	
177	        return bool(self._get_friendly_name())
178	
179	    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
180	        """Return whether the lock entity reports an operational state."""
181	        state = self.hass.states.get(self.lock.entity_id)
182	        return not (state is None or state.state == "unavailable")
183	
184	    @callback
185	    def _process_z2m_device_payload(self, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
186	        """Apply device-topic JSON on the Home Assistant event loop."""
187	        action = payload.get("action")
188	
189	        # Handle lock/unlock actions with user identification (keypad PIN usage)
190	        if isinstance(action, str) and action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS:
191	            action_user = payload.get("action_user")
192	            if action_user is not None and not isinstance(action_user, bool):
193	                try:
194	                    code_slot = int(action_user)
195	                except ValueError, TypeError:
196	                    LOGGER.debug(
197	                        "Ignoring %s with non-numeric action_user %r for %s",
198	                        action,
199	                        action_user,
200	                        self.lock.entity_id,
201	                    )
202	                    return
203	                to_locked = action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_LOCKED
204	                self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
205	                    code_slot=code_slot,
206	                    to_locked=to_locked,
207	                    action_text=action,
208	                    source_data=payload,
209	                )
210	            return
211	
212	        # Handle pin_code added / deleted (Z2M action events, not the users object)
213	        if action in ("pin_code_added", "pin_code_deleted"):
214	            action_user = payload.get("action_user")

content.subject: Close #1127 HA group bump
content.description: Close PR with a comment explaining the upstream incompatibility: HA 2026.5.x's matter.button.py references HeimanCluster.Commands.MutingSensor but matter-server's HeimanCluster has no Commands subclass — wait for an HA point release that drops/guards the reference, then dependabot will reopen

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- HA migrated from `python-matter-server` to **`matter-python-client`** in commit `6fba886edb5` (Feb 23, 2026). The new package bundles its own `chip/clusters/cluster_defs/` — including a `HeimanCluster.py` that **does** have `Commands.MutingSensor`.
- HA 2026.5.x still requires the new client (the manifest was updated), but our integration is pinned to the **old** `python-matter-server==8.1.2`. When HA 2026.5.0 loads its matter component, it imports `from chip.clusters.cluster_defs.HeimanCluster import HeimanCluster` — and resolves to *our* `python-matter-server`'s `chip` module, which lacks `Commands`. That's the failure.
- Fix: swap `python-matter-server` for `matter-python-client` in our requirements, and update any imports in our code that touch matter_server's API surface.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me find our actual matter_server / python-matter-server usage.

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- `matter-python-client` provides a `matter_server` module that's API-compatible with our import paths — every symbol we use (EventType, MatterNode, SCHEMA_VERSION, ServerInfoMessage, MatterNodeData, MatterNodeEvent, dataclass_from_dict) exists at the same path. So the swap is just a requirements edit; no code changes needed.
- HA's dependabot grouped `python-matter-server` and bumped HA, but not matter-server — because matter-server was renamed (not just bumped). Dependabot can't detect that semantic move. We have to do it manually.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me check the actual matter.py provider in our integration to confirm no other usages, then make the swap.

4	Handles PIN credential management via Matter lock helpers.
5	PINs are write-only: occupied slots report SlotCode.UNREADABLE_CODE, cleared slots report
6	SlotCode.EMPTY. Subscribes to DoorLock cluster events via the push framework for
7	code slot tracking (LockOperation) and occupancy updates (LockUserChange).
8	"""

"""
Matter lock provider.

Handles PIN credential management via Matter lock helpers.
PINs are write-only: occupied slots report SlotCode.UNREADABLE_CODE, cleared slots report
SlotCode.EMPTY. Subscribes to DoorLock cluster events via the push framework for
code slot tracking (LockOperation) and occupancy updates (LockUserChange).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import timedelta
from typing import Any, Literal

from matter_server.common.models import EventType

from homeassistant.components.matter.helpers import (
    get_matter,
    get_node_from_device_entry,
)
from homeassistant.components.matter.lock_helpers import (
    SetCredentialFailedError,
    clear_lock_credential,
    get_lock_credential_status,
    get_lock_info,
    get_lock_users,
    set_lock_credential,
    set_lock_user,

content.text: Re-running pre-commit after the auto-sort.

input.command:
git add requirements_dev.txt .github/dependabot.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: swap python-matter-server for matter-python-client

HA migrated its matter integration from python-matter-server to
matter-python-client in 2026.5.x. The new client bundles its own
chip.clusters.cluster_defs (including HeimanCluster.Commands.MutingSensor
which 2026.5.x's matter/button.py imports). Keeping the old python-matter-server
pin made HA's matter component fail to import at test-collection time.

matter-python-client re-exposes the same matter_server.* module surface we
use (EventType, MatterNode, SCHEMA_VERSION, ServerInfoMessage, MatterNodeData,
MatterNodeEvent, dataclass_from_dict), so no code changes are needed — just
the requirement swap.

Also rename python-matter-server to matter-python-client in the dependabot
homeassistant group so future bumps stay grouped together.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin dependabot/pip/homeassistant-6319076cf5 2>&1 | tail -10

Meanwhile, summary of what's been done so far:

**Three of three PRs fixed (not closed):**

| PR | Issue | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| #1126 mypy 2.0 | `user_id` reassigned from `int` to `Any\|None` | Rename raw `.get()` result to `raw_user`; only assign `user_id` via `int()`. **Mypy green.** |
| #1125 babel/preset-env | Stale `lock-code-manager.js` after minifier nudge | `yarn build`, commit regenerated bundle. **Frontend green.** |
| #1127 HA 2026.5.0 group | HA migrated `python-matter-server` → `matter-python-client` (new package, bundles `chip/clusters/cluster_defs/` with `HeimanCluster.Commands`) | Swap the requirement; update dependabot group patterns to track the new name. Imports verified locally. **Awaiting CI.** |

The HA migration was the interesting one — your local HA clone showed manifest commit `6fba886edb5` swapping the matter dependency in Feb 2026, which the dependabot homeassistant-group bot couldn't follow because it's a rename, not a version bump.

content.text:
No, I didn't — my verification used an ephemeral `uv run --with matter-python-client==0.6.0` venv, which doesn't persist.

The project's `.venv/` still has:
- `python-matter-server 8.1.2` (old, pinned by main)
- `matter-python-client 0.4.1` (older, probably a leftover transitive)

This is actually the **correct** state for the docs branch / main right now — main is still on `homeassistant>=2026.4.4` and `python-matter-server==8.1.2`. The swap to `matter-python-client==0.6.0` only takes effect when the HA-group PR merges and main moves to HA 2026.5.0.

If you'd like local parity with the HA-group PR (e.g. to run tests yourself before it merges), I can:

```bash
.venv/bin/uv pip install --upgrade matter-python-client==0.6.0
.venv/bin/uv pip uninstall python-matter-server
```

But I'd suggest holding off until that PR merges — otherwise the venv will be in a "future" state that doesn't match main, and any local test runs against the docs branch (or anything based on main) will be misleading. CI on the PR is what verifies the pin works end-to-end.

Want me to sync now anyway?

---
"""ESPHome serial proxy transport.

The `ESPHome serial proxy component <https://esphome.io/components/serial_proxy/>`_
exposes any ESPHome serial instance through the ESPHome API. This is used by serialx to
create a fully functional remote serial port, similar to RFC2217 but with many quality
of life, performance, and security improvements.

This platform requires the `aioesphomeapi <https://github.com/esphome/aioesphomeapi>`_
package to be installed, provided by the ``esphome`` dependency group:

.. code-block:: bash

    pip install 'serialx[esphome]'

.. note::
    The aioesphomeapi package does not provide a synchronous interface so the sync
    serial transport relies on spawning an asyncio event loop in a secondary thread in
    order to proxy the async methods. This interface is far from ideal. If you'd like to
    make use of this transport, please migrate your application to the async APIs for
    better compatibility.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from collections.abc import Callable, Coroutine
from contextlib import suppress
from enum import IntFlag
import functools
import logging
import threading
from typing import Any, ParamSpec, TypeVar, cast
import urllib.parse
import warnings

import aioesphomeapi
from aioesphomeapi import APIClient, SerialProxyDataReceived, SerialProxyParity
from aioesphomeapi.core import (
    APIConnectionError,
    PingRequest,
    PingResponse,
    TimeoutAPIError,
)
from typing_extensions import Buffer, Unpack

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- HA's `usb` component now imports `serialx.platforms.serial_esphome` at top level (via `serial_proxy_stub`), and that submodule has an unconditional `import aioesphomeapi` — but aioesphomeapi is only a serialx *extra* (`serialx[esphome]`). So pytest can't even collect tests when ZHA/USB are in the import chain unless aioesphomeapi is independently installed. HA 2026.5.0 pins `aioesphomeapi==44.21.0`.
- `hacs.json` currently declares min HA 2026.4.0. Since matter-python-client only exists in HA 2026.5+, the HACS minimum should be bumped to 2026.5.0 to match.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

content.text:
Pushed. Both changes addressed:

- **HACS minimum HA bumped to 2026.5.0** in `hacs.json` (since `matter-python-client` only ships in HA 2026.5+, that's now the floor — matches what the matter-server library swap implies)
- **`aioesphomeapi==44.21.0` added to `requirements_dev.txt`** — same version HA 2026.5.0 itself pins. Needed because HA's `usb` component now unconditionally imports `serialx.platforms.serial_esphome`, which top-level imports `aioesphomeapi`, but that's only a serialx optional extra (`serialx[esphome]`). Without this, pytest can't even collect tests once ZHA → homeassistant_hardware → usb gets pulled in.

Letting the next CI run for #1127 settle — the earlier scheduled wakeup will hit shortly and re-check.

content.content:
async def test_usercode_cc_version_missing(
    zwave_js_lock: ZWaveJSLock,
    caplog: pytest.LogCaptureFixture,
) -> None:
    """Test warning when User Code CC is not found on node."""
    # Remove User Code CC from the node's command classes
    node = zwave_js_lock.node
    endpoint = node.endpoints[0]
    original_ccs = endpoint.data["commandClasses"]
    endpoint.data["commandClasses"] = [
        cc for cc in original_ccs if cc["id"] != CommandClass.USER_CODE.value
    ]
    # Clear cached property so it re-evaluates
    zwave_js_lock.__dict__.pop("_usercode_cc_version", None)

    assert zwave_js_lock._usercode_cc_version == 1
    assert "User Code CC not found" in caplog.text


async def test_node_property(
    zwave_js_lock: ZWaveJSLock,
    lock_schlage_be469: Node,
) -> None:
    """Test node property returns the correct Z-Wave node."""
    node = zwave_js_lock.node
    assert node.node_id == lock_schlage_be469.node_id


# Connection tests

content.text: The fix is clear: zwave-js-server-python 0.70+ added `@cached_property` to `endpoint.command_classes`. The test modifies `endpoint.data["commandClasses"]` directly but doesn't invalidate the new cache. Let me also check the 57 mqtt errors.

async def mqtt_lock_entity(hass: HomeAssistant) -> er.RegistryEntry:
    """
    Create an MQTT config entry, Z2M device, and lock entity.

    Sets the MQTT config entry to LOADED state so the provider's
    async_is_integration_connected check passes.
    """
    mqtt_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="mqtt")
    mqtt_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED, None)

    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)

    device = dev_reg.async_get_or_create(
        config_entry_id=mqtt_entry.entry_id,
        connections=set(),
        identifiers={("mqtt", "zigbee2mqtt_0xc0ffee")},
        name=Z2M_TOPIC_NAME,
    )

    lock_entity = ent_reg.async_get_or_create(
        "lock",
        "mqtt",
        "test_z2m",
        config_entry=mqtt_entry,
        device_id=device.id,
    )

    hass.states.async_set(lock_entity.entity_id, "locked")

    return lock_entity


@pytest.fixture
async def lcm_config_entry(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    mqtt_lock_entity: er.RegistryEntry,
    mqtt_patches,
) -> MockConfigEntry:
    """
    Set up a full LCM config entry managing the Z2M lock.

    This goes through the real async_setup_entry path: LCM discovers the
    lock entity is from the mqtt platform, instantiates Zigbee2MQTTLock,
    creates the coordinator, entities, and sync managers.
    """
    entity_id = mqtt_lock_entity.entity_id
    config = {
        CONF_LOCKS: [entity_id],

content.text: Let me check HA 2026.5.0's MQTT unload behavior and how the existing fixture pokes state.

content.content:
async def async_remove_config_entry_device(
    hass: HomeAssistant, config_entry: ConfigEntry, device_entry: DeviceEntry
) -> bool:
    """Remove MQTT config entry from a device."""
    from . import device_automation  # noqa: PLC0415

    await device_automation.async_removed_from_device(hass, device_entry.id)
    return True


async def async_unload_entry(hass: HomeAssistant, entry: ConfigEntry) -> bool:
    """Unload MQTT dump and publish service when the config entry is unloaded."""
    mqtt_data = hass.data[DATA_MQTT]
    mqtt_client = mqtt_data.client

    # Stop the discovery
    await discovery.async_stop(hass)
    # Unload the platforms
    await hass.config_entries.async_unload_platforms(entry, mqtt_data.platforms_loaded)
    mqtt_data.platforms_loaded = set()
    await asyncio.sleep(0)
    # Unsubscribe reload dispatchers
    while reload_dispatchers := mqtt_data.reload_dispatchers:
        reload_dispatchers.pop()()
    # Cleanup listeners
    mqtt_client.cleanup()

    # Cleanup entity registry hooks
    registry_hooks = mqtt_data.discovery_registry_hooks
    while registry_hooks:
        registry_hooks.popitem()[1]()
    # Wait for all ACKs, stop the loop and disconnect the client
    await mqtt_client.async_disconnect(disconnect_paho_client=True)

    # Cleanup MQTT client availability
    hass.data.pop(DATA_MQTT_AVAILABLE, None)
    # Store remaining subscriptions to be able to restore or reload them
    # when the entry is set up again
    if subscriptions := mqtt_client.subscriptions:
        mqtt_data.subscriptions_to_restore = subscriptions

    # Unsubscribe reload dispatchers

    await mqtt_client.async_disconnect(disconnect_paho_client=True)

    hass.data.pop(DATA_MQTT_AVAILABLE, None)

content.text:
Both failures are scoped:
- **1 test failure** (zwave_js cache invalidation) — 1-line test fix
- **57 teardown errors** — all from one fixture in `tests/providers/zigbee2mqtt/conftest.py` that force-marks a `MockConfigEntry(domain="mqtt")` as LOADED but never populates `hass.data["mqtt"]`. HA 2026.5.0's `mqtt.async_unload_entry` no longer guards that access.

Let me apply both fixes.

async def test_usercode_cc_version_missing(
    zwave_js_lock: ZWaveJSLock,
    caplog: pytest.LogCaptureFixture,
) -> None:
    """Test warning when User Code CC is not found on node."""
    # Remove User Code CC from the node's command classes
    node = zwave_js_lock.node
    endpoint = node.endpoints[0]
    original_ccs = endpoint.data["commandClasses"]
    endpoint.data["commandClasses"] = [
        cc for cc in original_ccs if cc["id"] != CommandClass.USER_CODE.value
    ]
    # Clear cached property so it re-evaluates
    zwave_js_lock.__dict__.pop("_usercode_cc_version", None)

    assert zwave_js_lock._usercode_cc_version == 1
    assert "User Code CC not found" in caplog.text

    """

    Sets the MQTT config entry to LOADED state so the provider's

"""Shared fixtures and constants for Zigbee2MQTT provider tests."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import json
from types import SimpleNamespace
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest
from pytest_homeassistant_custom_component.common import MockConfigEntry

from homeassistant.components.mqtt.models import ReceiveMessage
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntryState
from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
from homeassistant.helpers import device_registry as dr, entity_registry as er

from custom_components.lock_code_manager.const import (
    CONF_LOCKS,
    CONF_SLOTS,
    DOMAIN,
)
from custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt import (
    Zigbee2MQTTLock,
)

Z2M_TOPIC_NAME = "TestLockZ2M"
Z2M_FULL_TOPIC = f"zigbee2mqtt/{Z2M_TOPIC_NAME}"
Z2M_GET_TOPIC = f"{Z2M_FULL_TOPIC}/get"
Z2M_SET_TOPIC = f"{Z2M_FULL_TOPIC}/set"
Z2M_LOCK_UNIQUE_ID = "test_z2m"


@dataclass
class MqttMessageBus:
    """
    Lightweight MQTT message bus for E2E testing.

    Tracks subscriptions and publishes so tests can fire incoming messages
    and verify outgoing publishes without a real Paho client.
    """

    subscriptions: dict[str, list[Callable]] = field(default_factory=dict)
    publishes: list[tuple[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)

    def subscribe(self, topic: str, callback: Callable) -> Callable[[], None]:
        """Register a subscription callback and return an unsubscribe handle."""
        self.subscriptions.setdefault(topic, []).append(callback)

        def unsub() -> None:
            self.subscriptions.get(topic, []).remove(callback)

        return unsub

    async def publish(
        self,
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        topic: str,
        payload: str,
        **kwargs: Any,
    ) -> None:
        """Record a published message."""
        self.publishes.append((topic, payload))

    def fire_message(self, topic: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """
        Simulate an incoming MQTT message on a topic.

        Creates a ReceiveMessage-like object and dispatches to all
        registered callbacks for the topic.
        """
        msg = MagicMock(spec=ReceiveMessage)
        msg.topic = topic
        msg.payload = json.dumps(payload).encode()
        msg.qos = 0
        msg.retain = False
        for cb in self.subscriptions.get(topic, []):
            cb(msg)


def _minimal_lock() -> Zigbee2MQTTLock:
    """Build a Zigbee2MQTTLock without Home Assistant test harness."""
    lock_entity = SimpleNamespace(
        entity_id="lock.test",
        device_id=None,
        platform="mqtt",
        config_entry_id=None,
        unique_id=None,
    )
    return Zigbee2MQTTLock(
        MagicMock(),
        MagicMock(),
        MagicMock(),
        None,
        lock_entity,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )


@pytest.fixture
def mqtt_bus() -> MqttMessageBus:
    """Create a fresh MQTT message bus for the test."""
    return MqttMessageBus()


@pytest.fixture
def mqtt_patches(mqtt_bus: MqttMessageBus):
    """
    Patch async_subscribe and async_publish at the HA MQTT component boundary.

    Also patches mqtt_config_entry_enabled to return True so the provider
    does not bail out during setup.
    """

    async def fake_subscribe(hass, topic, callback, *args, **kwargs):
        return mqtt_bus.subscribe(topic, callback)

    async def fake_publish(hass, topic, payload, *args, **kwargs):
        await mqtt_bus.publish(hass, topic, payload, **kwargs)
        # Auto-respond to GET requests so the coordinator's initial
        # refresh doesn't block on 10-second timeouts per slot.
        if topic.endswith("/get"):
            try:
                body = json.loads(payload)
                slot = body.get("pin_code", {}).get("user")
                if slot is not None:
                    device_topic = topic.rsplit("/get", 1)[0]
                    mqtt_bus.fire_message(
                        device_topic,
                        {
                            "pin_code": {
                                "user": slot,
                                "user_enabled": False,
                                "pin_code": None,
                            }
                        },
                    )
            except json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError:
                pass

    with (
        patch(
            "custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt.async_subscribe",
            side_effect=fake_subscribe,
        ),
        patch(
            "custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt.async_publish",
            side_effect=fake_publish,
        ),
        patch(
            "custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt.mqtt_config_entry_enabled",
            return_value=True,
        ),
    ):
        yield

async def mqtt_lock_entity(hass: HomeAssistant) -> er.RegistryEntry:
    """
    Create an MQTT config entry, Z2M device, and lock entity.

    Sets the MQTT config entry to LOADED state so the provider's
    async_is_integration_connected check passes.
    """
    mqtt_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="mqtt")
    mqtt_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED, None)

    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)

    device = dev_reg.async_get_or_create(
        config_entry_id=mqtt_entry.entry_id,
        connections=set(),
        identifiers={("mqtt", "zigbee2mqtt_0xc0ffee")},
        name=Z2M_TOPIC_NAME,
    )

    lock_entity = ent_reg.async_get_or_create(
        "lock",
        "mqtt",
        "test_z2m",
        config_entry=mqtt_entry,
        device_id=device.id,
    )

    hass.states.async_set(lock_entity.entity_id, "locked")

    return lock_entity


@pytest.fixture
async def lcm_config_entry(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    mqtt_lock_entity: er.RegistryEntry,
    mqtt_patches,
) -> MockConfigEntry:
    """
    Set up a full LCM config entry managing the Z2M lock.

    This goes through the real async_setup_entry path: LCM discovers the
    lock entity is from the mqtt platform, instantiates Zigbee2MQTTLock,
    creates the coordinator, entities, and sync managers.
    """
    entity_id = mqtt_lock_entity.entity_id
    config = {
        CONF_LOCKS: [entity_id],
        CONF_SLOTS: {
            1: {"name": "slot1", "pin": "1234", "enabled": True},
            2: {"name": "slot2", "pin": "5678", "enabled": True},
        },
    }
    lcm_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain=DOMAIN, data=config, unique_id="test_z2m_e2e")
    lcm_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
    assert await hass.config_entries.async_setup(lcm_entry.entry_id)
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    yield lcm_entry

    await hass.config_entries.async_unload(lcm_entry.entry_id)


def get_z2m_lock(lcm_entry: MockConfigEntry) -> Zigbee2MQTTLock:
    """Extract the Zigbee2MQTTLock from a loaded LCM config entry."""
    locks = lcm_entry.runtime_data.locks
    assert len(locks) == 1, f"Expected 1 lock, found {len(locks)}"
    lock = next(iter(locks.values()))
    assert isinstance(lock, Zigbee2MQTTLock)
    return lock


@pytest.fixture
def z2m_lock(hass, lcm_config_entry):
    """Extract the Z2M lock from the LCM config entry."""
    return get_z2m_lock(lcm_config_entry)

@pytest.fixture
async def mqtt_lock_entity(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
) -> AsyncGenerator[er.RegistryEntry, None]:
    """
    Create an MQTT config entry, Z2M device, and lock entity.

    Sets the MQTT config entry to LOADED state so the provider's
    async_is_integration_connected check passes. Resets the state to
    NOT_LOADED on teardown because HA 2026.5.0's mqtt.async_unload_entry
    does an unguarded ``hass.data[DATA_MQTT]`` lookup that this fake
    setup never populates.
    """
    mqtt_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="mqtt")
    mqtt_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED, None)

    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)

    device = dev_reg.async_get_or_create(
        config_entry_id=mqtt_entry.entry_id,
        connections=set(),
        identifiers={("mqtt", "zigbee2mqtt_0xc0ffee")},
        name=Z2M_TOPIC_NAME,
    )

    lock_entity = ent_reg.async_get_or_create(
        "lock",
        "mqtt",
        "test_z2m",
        config_entry=mqtt_entry,
        device_id=device.id,
    )

    hass.states.async_set(lock_entity.entity_id, "locked")

    yield lock_entity

    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.NOT_LOADED, None)

async def mqtt_lock_entity(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
) -> AsyncGenerator[er.RegistryEntry, None]:
    """
    Create an MQTT config entry, Z2M device, and lock entity.

    Sets the MQTT config entry to LOADED state so the provider's
    async_is_integration_connected check passes. Resets the state to
    NOT_LOADED on teardown because HA 2026.5.0's mqtt.async_unload_entry
    does an unguarded ``hass.data[DATA_MQTT]`` lookup that this fake
    setup never populates.
    """
    mqtt_entry = MockConfigEntry(domain="mqtt")
    mqtt_entry.add_to_hass(hass)
    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.LOADED, None)

    dev_reg = dr.async_get(hass)
    ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)

    device = dev_reg.async_get_or_create(
        config_entry_id=mqtt_entry.entry_id,
        connections=set(),
        identifiers={("mqtt", "zigbee2mqtt_0xc0ffee")},
        name=Z2M_TOPIC_NAME,
    )

    lock_entity = ent_reg.async_get_or_create(
        "lock",
        "mqtt",
        "test_z2m",
        config_entry=mqtt_entry,
        device_id=device.id,
    )

    hass.states.async_set(lock_entity.entity_id, "locked")

    yield lock_entity

    mqtt_entry._async_set_state(hass, ConfigEntryState.NOT_LOADED, None)
